concept_name: Which Way Does the Force Point? — the Right-Hand Rule for F = q v × B
source_book: DC Pandey Class 12 Ch.4 Moving Charges and Magnetism — table-of-contents reference only (the 'force on a moving charge' heading that precedes the magnitude lesson); teaching authored from first principles. NCERT consulted for the Indian real-world anchor only. This concept is the DIRECTION-only sibling of magnetic_force_moving_charge: it teaches HOW to find which way the magnetic force points using the right-hand rule for F = q v × B, and nothing about its size. The atomic claim: the magnetic force on a moving charge is perpendicular to BOTH the velocity v and the field B at the same time; the right hand chooses which of the two perpendicular directions, and a negative charge flips the answer by 180 degrees. It deliberately does NOT teach the magnitude F = qvB sin(theta), the circular-motion radius r = mv/qB, the period, or any Newton/Tesla number — those belong to magnetic_force_moving_charge and are NEVER shown on-canvas here. Conceptual-only ship (no mode_overrides). EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; misconceptions confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch + predict→reveal). Renders on field_3d via the rhr_force_direction scenario (the direction-only sibling of lorentz_force_uniform_field), which shares the 3D right-hand mesh (extras.palm_rule.show_3d_hand). Lighter atom by founder carve-out: no assessment block, no coverage_map.

real_world_anchor.primary: Hold a strong magnet near the glass of an old CRT television or a school-lab CRT monitor while a bright picture is showing. The picture warps and the colours smear in a curved swirl. Inside that tube a beam of electrons is racing toward the screen, and the magnet's field is shoving each moving electron sideways — bending the whole beam. Which way the picture warps depends entirely on which way that sideways magnetic force points, and that is exactly what the right-hand rule tells you. Move the magnet to the other side and the warp swings the other way.
real_world_anchor.secondary: At the VECC cyclotron in Kolkata, charged particles are whipped round and round and steered along their path by carefully aimed magnetic forces. Every bend in their track is a magnetic force pushing sideways on a moving charge — the same q v × B direction rule, scaled up to a research machine that accelerates beams for medicine and physics.

v.name: Speed of the moving charge (direction-only here; the number is never shown)

B.name: Magnetic field strength (direction-only here; the number is never shown)

theta_deg.name: Angle between v and B (controls only whether a sideways force exists)

q_sign.name: Sign of the charge: +1 positive, -1 negative (flips the force direction)

formulas.force_is_perpendicular_to_velocity: The magnetic force is always perpendicular to the velocity — it never points along the motion
formulas.force_is_perpendicular_to_field: The magnetic force is also perpendicular to the field — it is at right angles to BOTH v and B at once
formulas.right_hand_picks_the_side: Point the right-hand fingers along v, curl them through the smaller angle toward B, and the thumb gives the direction of the force for a positive charge
formulas.negative_charge_flips_it: For a negative charge the force points the opposite way — a full 180 degrees from the positive-charge answer
formulas.no_push_along_the_field: When v points along B there is no sideways force at all; only the part of v across the field gets pushed

constraints: This concept teaches the DIRECTION of the magnetic force only — it never shows a magnitude number, F = qvB sin(theta), the radius r = mv/qB, the period, or any Newton or Tesla value
The magnetic force is perpendicular to the velocity v at every instant
The magnetic force is perpendicular to the field B at every instant
Because the force is q times (v cross B), a negative charge reverses the direction by 180 degrees while v and B stay the same
When v is parallel to B (theta = 0 or 180 degrees) or the charge is at rest, there is no magnetic force — the F arrow is hidden, never a zero-length stub
theta_deg controls only whether a sideways force exists and how far across the field v lies — it is direction information, not a magnitude readout
The right-hand rule gives v cross B; the force on the actual charge is q times that, so the charge's sign decides the final side

aha_moment.statement: F points square to both v and B; the right hand picks the side.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: Right-angle marks prove F is perpendicular to both v and B; STATE_4 then flips F a full 180 degrees for a negative charge.

scene_composition.text: a charge speeds through a magnetic field

it does NOT go straight —
which way is it pushed?
a sideways force appears — across the motion, not along it

tts_sentences.text_en: A charge is moving through a magnetic field. Watch its path.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक charge magnetic field से गुज़र रहा है। इसका path देखिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక charge magnetic field లో కదులుతోంది. దాని path చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: It doesn't go straight — something shoves it sideways. Which way will the push point?
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह straight नहीं जाता — कुछ इसे sideways धकेलता है। push किस direction में होगी?
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది straight గా వెళ్ళదు — ఏదో దాన్ని sideways తోస్తోంది. push ఏ direction లో ఉంటుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: A force appears — pointing sideways, across the motion, not along it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक force प्रकट होती है — sideways, motion के पार, उसके साथ नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక force కనిపిస్తుంది — sideways గా, motion కు అడ్డంగా, దాని వెంట కాదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: This sideways shove is the magnetic force. The rest of this lesson is: which way does it point?
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह sideways धक्का ही magnetic force है। इस lesson का बाकी हिस्सा यही है: यह किस direction में point करती है?
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ sideways తోపు అంటే magnetic force. ఈ lesson లో మిగిలినది ఇదే: ఇది ఏ direction లో point చేస్తుంది?

misconception_watch.belief: The magnetic force pushes the charge along its motion and speeds it up
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The force arrow pops out sideways, square across the velocity arrow — never forward along the path.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The magnetic force is square to the motion — it steers the charge sideways, it never pushes it forward.

STATE_2.title: The Rule — fingers v, curl to B, thumb is F

scene_composition.text: right hand: point fingers along v

Predict: which way
is your thumb pointing?
thumb points where the FORCE points

tts_sentences.text_en: Here's the rule. Open your right hand. Point your fingers along v, the way the charge moves.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह रहा rule। अपना right hand खोलिए। अपनी fingers को v की direction में, यानी charge जिस तरफ जा रहा है, उधर point कीजिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది rule. మీ right hand తెరవండి. మీ fingers ను v దిశలో, అంటే charge వెళ్ళే దిశలో, point చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now curl your fingers through the smaller angle, toward B, the field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब अपनी fingers को छोटे angle से B, यानी field, की तरफ मोड़िए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీ fingers ను చిన్న angle గుండా B, అంటే field, వైపు వంచండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Your thumb now points one specific way. Before I show it — which way is your thumb pointing?
tts_sentences.text_hi: आपका thumb अब एक specific direction में point कर रहा है। मैं बताने से पहले — आपका thumb किस तरफ है?
tts_sentences.text_te: మీ thumb ఇప్పుడు ఒక specific direction లో point చేస్తోంది. నేను చూపించే ముందు — మీ thumb ఏ వైపు ఉంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: Your thumb gives the direction of the force. The force points where the thumb points.
tts_sentences.text_hi: आपका thumb force की direction देता है। force उसी तरफ point करती है जहाँ thumb point करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీ thumb force దిశను ఇస్తుంది. force అక్కడే point చేస్తుంది, ఎక్కడ thumb point చేస్తుందో.

tts_sentences.text_en: Release the charge — it gets pushed exactly the way the thumb showed.
tts_sentences.text_hi: charge को release कीजिए — यह ठीक उसी direction में push होती है जो thumb ने दिखाई थी।
tts_sentences.text_te: charge ను release చేయండి — ఇది thumb చూపించిన దిశలోనే push అవుతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: The thumb shows how fast or how hard the charge is pushed
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The thumb is just a pointer for the force direction — nothing about its length stands for speed or strength.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The thumb only picks the DIRECTION of the force — it is a pointer, never a throttle.

STATE_3.title: Square to BOTH — F is perpendicular to v and to B

scene_composition.text: look at the angle between F and v

right angle to v
AND right angle to B

F stands straight out of the
flat sheet that holds v and B

tts_sentences.text_en: Look at the force arrow against the velocity arrow. What angle is between them?
tts_sentences.text_hi: force arrow को velocity arrow के सामने देखिए। इनके बीच कौन सा angle है?
tts_sentences.text_te: force arrow ను velocity arrow తో పోల్చి చూడండి. వాటి మధ్య ఏ angle ఉంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: A right angle. The force is exactly perpendicular to the velocity.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक right angle। force बिल्कुल velocity के perpendicular है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక right angle. force సరిగ్గా velocity కు perpendicular గా ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: And it's also perpendicular to the field. The force is at right angles to BOTH at once.
tts_sentences.text_hi: और यह field के भी perpendicular है। force एक साथ दोनों के right angles पर है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మరియు ఇది field కు కూడా perpendicular గా ఉంది. force ఒకేసారి రెండింటికీ right angles లో ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Let me tilt the view: F stands straight out of the flat sheet that holds v and B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: मैं view को tilt करता हूँ: F उस flat sheet से बिल्कुल सीधा बाहर निकलता है जिसमें v और B हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: నేను view ను tilt చేస్తాను: F అనేది v మరియు B ను కలిగి ఉన్న flat sheet నుండి నేరుగా బయటకు వస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The magnetic force never points along the motion. It always points out to the side, square to both v and B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: magnetic force कभी भी motion के साथ point नहीं करती। यह हमेशा side में point करती है, v और B दोनों के square होकर।
tts_sentences.text_te: magnetic force ఎప్పుడూ motion వెంట point చేయదు. ఇది ఎల్లప్పుడూ side కు point చేస్తుంది, v మరియు B రెండింటికీ square గా.

misconception_watch.belief: The force lies somewhere in the same plane as v and B, leaning between them
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Tilting the view shows F standing straight out of the v–B sheet, with right-angle marks on both v and B.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: F is square to BOTH v and B — it sticks out of their plane, it never leans between them.

STATE_4.title: Flip the Sign — a negative charge reverses F

scene_composition.text: same v, same B — but now the charge is negative

Predict: force the SAME way,
or the OPPOSITE way?
opposite — a negative charge flips F a full 180°

tts_sentences.text_en: Same velocity, same field as before. But now the charge is negative — an electron, say.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वही velocity, वही field जैसा पहले था। लेकिन अब charge negative है — मान लीजिए एक electron।
tts_sentences.text_te: అదే velocity, అదే field ముందులా. కానీ ఇప్పుడు charge negative — ఒక electron అని అనుకుందాం.

tts_sentences.text_en: Run the same right-hand rule: fingers along v, curl to B, thumb out. Where does the force point now — same way, or opposite?
tts_sentences.text_hi: वही right-hand rule चलाइए: fingers v के साथ, B की तरफ curl, thumb बाहर। force अब किस direction में point करती है — same तरफ, या opposite?
tts_sentences.text_te: అదే right-hand rule వాడండి: fingers v వెంట, B వైపు curl, thumb బయటకు. force ఇప్పుడు ఏ direction లో point చేస్తుంది — అదే వైపు, లేదా opposite వైపు?

tts_sentences.text_en: Opposite. For a negative charge the force flips a full 180 degrees.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Opposite। एक negative charge के लिए force पूरे 180 degrees पलट जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Opposite. ఒక negative charge కోసం force పూర్తిగా 180 degrees తిరుగుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The right-hand rule still gives you v cross B — but the force is q times that, so a negative q turns it around.
tts_sentences.text_hi: right-hand rule अब भी v cross B देता है — लेकिन force q times वह है, इसलिए एक negative q उसे पलट देता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: right-hand rule ఇప్పటికీ మీకు v cross B ఇస్తుంది — కానీ force అనేది q times అది, కాబట్టి negative q దాన్ని తిప్పుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same hand, same gesture. Only the sign of the charge decided the side.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वही hand, वही gesture। सिर्फ charge के sign ने side तय किया।
tts_sentences.text_te: అదే hand, అదే gesture. charge యొక్క sign మాత్రమే side ను నిర్ణయించింది.

misconception_watch.belief: The right-hand rule gives the same force direction whatever the sign of the charge
misconception_watch.visual_counter: With the same v and B, the F arrow flips a full 180 degrees for a negative charge — a fading ghost marks where the positive-charge force pointed.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Negative charge → reverse the thumb. Same rule, opposite answer.

STATE_5.title: Into the Page — ⊗ and ⊙ are the same hand

scene_composition.text: ⊗ = into the page    ⊙ = out of the page

same rule: fingers along v,
curl into the page toward B

a flat ⊗ / ⊙ diagram is just
our 3D rule seen end-on

tts_sentences.text_en: In textbooks the field often points straight into the page. We draw that as a cross in a circle.
tts_sentences.text_hi: textbooks में field अक्सर सीधे page के अंदर point करती है। हम उसे circle में cross के रूप में draw करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: textbooks లో field తరచూ నేరుగా page లోపలికి point చేస్తుంది. దాన్ని మనం circle లో cross గా draw చేస్తాం.

tts_sentences.text_en: A cross is the tail of an arrow flying away from you — into the page. A dot is the tip coming toward you — out of the page.
tts_sentences.text_hi: cross उस arrow की tail है जो आपसे दूर जा रही है — page के अंदर। dot वह tip है जो आपकी तरफ आ रही है — page से बाहर।
tts_sentences.text_te: cross అంటే మీ నుండి దూరంగా వెళ్ళే arrow యొక్క tail — page లోపలికి. dot అంటే మీ వైపు వచ్చే tip — page బయటకు.

tts_sentences.text_en: It's the SAME rule. Point fingers along v, curl them into the page toward B, read the thumb.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह वही rule है। fingers को v के साथ point कीजिए, उन्हें page के अंदर B की तरफ curl कीजिए, thumb पढ़िए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది అదే rule. fingers ను v వెంట point చేయండి, వాటిని page లోపలికి B వైపు curl చేయండి, thumb చదవండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch what happens if I flip the cross to a dot — out of the page instead.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए क्या होता है जब मैं cross को dot में flip करता हूँ — page से बाहर की तरफ।
tts_sentences.text_te: నేను cross ను dot గా flip చేస్తే ఏమవుతుందో చూడండి — page బయటకు.

tts_sentences.text_en: The thumb swings the other way. A flat cross-in-circle or dot-in-circle diagram is just our 3D rule seen end-on.
tts_sentences.text_hi: thumb दूसरी तरफ swing करता है। एक flat cross-in-circle या dot-in-circle diagram बस हमारा 3D rule है जो end-on देखा गया है।
tts_sentences.text_te: thumb వేరే వైపు swing అవుతుంది. ఒక flat cross-in-circle లేదా dot-in-circle diagram అంటే మన 3D rule ని end-on చూసినట్టే.

misconception_watch.belief: The cross and dot symbols are just decoration, and a flat diagram can't be connected to the 3D right-hand rule
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The 3D hand re-orients its fingers INTO the page for the cross, and the force it gives matches the 2D answer exactly.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Cross means fingers into the page, dot means fingers out — then it is the same hand.

STATE_6.title: Along the Field — no shove, then explore

scene_composition.text: now v points along B — same direction

no sideways push at all —
the force is zero
swing the angle, change v, change B, flip the charge — watch which way F points

tts_sentences.text_en: One last case. Here the charge moves straight along the field — v and B point the same way.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक आखिरी case। यहाँ charge field के साथ-साथ straight चलती है — v और B एक ही direction में point करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక చివరి case. ఇక్కడ charge field వెంట straight గా కదులుతుంది — v మరియు B ఒకే direction లో point చేస్తున్నాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Try the right-hand rule. Fingers along v, curl toward B — but there's nothing to curl through. What force do you expect?
tts_sentences.text_hi: right-hand rule try कीजिए। fingers v के साथ, B की तरफ curl — लेकिन curl करने के लिए कुछ भी नहीं है। आप कौन सी force expect करते हैं?
tts_sentences.text_te: right-hand rule try చేయండి. fingers v వెంట, B వైపు curl — కానీ curl చేయడానికి ఏమీ లేదు. మీరు ఏ force expect చేస్తారు?

tts_sentences.text_en: None. When v lines up with B, there is no sideways push at all. The force is zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: कोई नहीं। जब v, B के साथ line up होता है, तो कोई sideways push नहीं होती। force zero है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఏమీ లేదు. v, B తో line up అయినప్పుడు, sideways push అసలు లేదు. force zero.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now you drive it. Swing the angle, change the speed, change the field, flip the charge — and watch which way the force points.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आप इसे drive कीजिए। angle swing कीजिए, speed बदलिए, field बदलिए, charge flip कीजिए — और देखिए force किस direction में point करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీరు drive చేయండి. angle swing చేయండి, speed మార్చండి, field మార్చండి, charge flip చేయండి — మరియు force ఏ direction లో point చేస్తుందో చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: As you turn v away from B, the force grows; bring it back in line, it shrinks to nothing.
tts_sentences.text_hi: जैसे-जैसे आप v को B से दूर घुमाते हैं, force बढ़ती है; उसे वापस line में लाइए, यह शून्य तक सिकुड़ जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీరు v ను B నుండి దూరంగా తిప్పుతున్నప్పుడు, force పెరుగుతుంది; దాన్ని తిరిగి line లోకి తెండి, అది సున్నాకి తగ్గిపోతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: There is always a magnetic force on a moving charge, no matter which way it moves
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Sliding v parallel to B makes the F arrow vanish; only when v tilts across B does a force reappear.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Move along B and the force is zero — only the part of v across the field gets pushed.

tts_sentences.text_en: A charge is moving through a magnetic field. Watch its path.
It doesn't go straight — something shoves it sideways. Which way will the push point?
A force appears — pointing sideways, across the motion, not along it.
This sideways shove is the magnetic force. The rest of this lesson is: which way does it point?

STATE_2.label: The rule — fingers v, curl to B, thumb is F

tts_sentences.text_en: Here's the rule. Open your right hand. Point your fingers along v, the way the charge moves.
Now curl your fingers through the smaller angle, toward B, the field.
Your thumb now points one specific way. Before I show it — which way is your thumb pointing?
Your thumb gives the direction of the force. The force points where the thumb points.
Release the charge — it gets pushed exactly the way the thumb showed.

STATE_3.label: Square to BOTH — F perpendicular to v and B

tts_sentences.text_en: Look at the force arrow against the velocity arrow. What angle is between them?
A right angle. The force is exactly perpendicular to the velocity.
And it's also perpendicular to the field. The force is at right angles to BOTH at once.
Let me tilt the view: F stands straight out of the flat sheet that holds v and B.
The magnetic force never points along the motion. It always points out to the side, square to both v and B.

STATE_4.label: Flip the sign — a negative charge reverses F

tts_sentences.text_en: Same velocity, same field as before. But now the charge is negative — an electron, say.
Run the same right-hand rule: fingers along v, curl to B, thumb out. Where does the force point now — same way, or opposite?
Opposite. For a negative charge the force flips a full 180 degrees.
The right-hand rule still gives you v cross B — but the force is q times that, so a negative q turns it around.
Same hand, same gesture. Only the sign of the charge decided the side.

STATE_5.label: Into the page — cross and dot are the same hand

tts_sentences.text_en: In textbooks the field often points straight into the page. We draw that as a cross in a circle.
A cross is the tail of an arrow flying away from you — into the page. A dot is the tip coming toward you — out of the page.
It's the SAME rule. Point fingers along v, curl them into the page toward B, read the thumb.
Watch what happens if I flip the cross to a dot — out of the page instead.
The thumb swings the other way. A flat cross-in-circle or dot-in-circle diagram is just our 3D rule seen end-on.

STATE_6.label: Along the field — no shove, then explore

tts_sentences.text_en: One last case. Here the charge moves straight along the field — v and B point the same way.
Try the right-hand rule. Fingers along v, curl toward B — but there's nothing to curl through. What force do you expect?
None. When v lines up with B, there is no sideways push at all. The force is zero.
Now you drive it. Swing the angle, change the speed, change the field, flip the charge — and watch which way the force points.
As you turn v away from B, the force grows; bring it back in line, it shrinks to nothing.
